title: Planet Labs Stock Forecast 2026: Can PL Sustain 468% Returns With $900M Defense Backlog?
text:
Planet Labs' stock has delivered extraordinary returns, appreciating 468% (and continuing to rise with 981.82% annual gains), raising the question of whether the company can sustain its growth trajectory with its $900 million defense backlog. The company's fiscal 2026 results and strategic position suggest strong fundamentals underpin the stock appreciation.

The $900 million backlog represents approximately three years of revenues at current run-rates, providing substantial visibility for growth. Approximately 85% of revenue comes from annual or multi-year contracts, offering predictability and stability. Major contracts include a multi-year, low nine-figure agreement with Sweden's Armed Forces, a €240 million German government contract, and additional agreements with NATO and other European partners.

Planet Labs' transition to profitability with $15.5 million adjusted EBITDA and $52.9 million free cash flow demonstrates operational maturity. The company's non-GAAP gross margins of 59% support its ability to leverage growth into operating leverage. Defense & Intelligence segment revenue grew over 50% year-over-year, suggesting government customers' demand for satellite intelligence continues to accelerate.

Fiscal 2027 guidance of $415-440 million revenue (35-43% growth) implies the company is pulling forward backlog while maintaining its profitable growth model. Analyst consensus rates PL as a Buy with average price targets around $35, suggesting further upside potential. However, execution risk remains as the company must balance satellite deployment cadence with demand growth and maintain its transition to sustainable profitability.
